name: equivalent
statement_text:
Two Runge–Kutta methods are ‘equivalent’ if, for any
initial value problem defined by an autonomous function f satisfying a
Lipschitz condition, and an initial value y0 , there exists h0 > 0 such that
the result computed by the first method is identical with the result computed
by the second method, if h ≤ h0 .
context_latex:
\paragraph{Context}
Consider an autonomous ODE $y' = f(y)$ with $f$ Lipschitz continuous. For two Runge--Kutta methods applied with initial value $y_0$ and step size $h$, let the computed results after one step be denoted $y_1$ and $\tilde y_1$, respectively.

variables.name: f
variables.definition: autonomous function f (right-hand side of ODE) satisfying a Lipschitz condition

preamble: The text discusses equivalence classes of Runge–Kutta methods, introducing three equivalence relations: 'equivalence', 'Φ-equivalence', and 'P-equivalence'. The definition given is for the first of these, termed 'equivalence', which compares the computed results of two methods for autonomous ODEs with Lipschitz conditions.

dependencies.name: Lipschitz condition in its second variable
